## Items In Scope

### Item 1 — Change default CodeLens preview length to 205

**Current state:** `codeLensMaxLength` defaults to `0` (no truncation) in `package.json` and `configuration.ts`.

**Change:** Set default to `205`. When `0`, description says "no truncation"; when `205`, preview is trimmed with `...` after 205 chars.

**Files:**
- `package.json` — change `"default": 0` to `"default": 205` for `kat-comment-studio.codeLensMaxLength`
- `src/configuration.ts` — change fallback `0` → `205` in `config.get<number>('codeLensMaxLength', 0)`
- `README.md` — update description of this setting to note 205 is the default

---

### Item 2 — See Also: bullet list → table format

**Current state:** `renderSeeAlsoSection()` builds a single `CommentSectionType.SeeAlso` section where each entry is a bullet-prefixed line (`• `) with a single `SegmentType.Link` segment. It is rendered via `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich()`.

**Change:** Render as a two-column table (Name | Description), matching the params/exceptions pattern.

#### Data model change
Add optional `nameLink?: string` to `RenderedCommentSection` in `src/types.ts`. This carries the command URI (for cref) or the href URL so the table renderer can build a clickable name cell.

#### Per-entry behavior
- **`cref` entry** (`<seealso cref="System.String"/>`):
  - Name cell: type name in blue/code style (using `katCommentStudio.typeName` VS Code color token), linked with `command:workbench.action.quickOpen?%5B%22%23{TypeName}%22%5D` so clicking opens VS Code quick symbol search pre-populated with `#{TypeName}`.
  - Description cell: inner text of the `<seealso>` element (empty if self-closing).
- **`href` entry** (`<seealso href="https://example.com">Link Text</seealso>`):
  - Name cell: the text `Visit Url` as a standard markdown link `[Visit Url](href)` — clicking opens the URL in the browser.
  - Description cell: the inner text of the element (i.e., `Link Text`).

#### Rendering change
- `renderSeeAlsoSection()` — change from one shared section with bullet lines to **one section per entry** (like exceptions), each with `name`, `nameLink`, and `lines` (description).
- Add `renderSeeAlsoTableMarkdown(sections)` function — builds a `| Name | Description |` table, analogous to `renderParamTableMarkdown()` and `renderExceptionTableMarkdown()`.
- In `renderToMarkdown()`, change the `CommentSectionType.SeeAlso` case to collect all SeeAlso sections and call `renderSeeAlsoTableMarkdown()` (same batching pattern as params/exceptions).
- Remove the existing `sectionLinesToMarkdownRich(section)` call for SeeAlso.

**Files:**
- `src/types.ts` — add `nameLink?: string` to `RenderedCommentSection`
- `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` — rewrite `renderSeeAlsoSection()`, add `renderSeeAlsoTableMarkdown()`, update `renderToMarkdown()` SeeAlso case
- `test/` — update/add tests for seealso table rendering (cref and href variants, with and without description)

---

### Item 3 — Remove All Comments: rename + confirmation dialog

**Current state:** `kat-comment-studio.removeAllComments` runs immediately with no confirmation. Label is "Remove All Comments".

**Change:**
- Rename to **"Remove All Comments in File"** (matching "Reflow Comments in File" naming convention).
- Add a `vscode.window.showWarningMessage()` confirmation prompt before executing: *"Remove all XML comments in this file?"* with **Remove** and **Cancel** buttons. Only proceed if user clicks **Remove**.

**Files:**
- `src/commands/commentRemover.ts` — add confirmation dialog, update command handler
- `package.json` — change `"title": "Remove All Comments"` → `"Remove All Comments in File"` in both the `commands` array and any `menus` entries
- `README.md` — update command documentation

---

### Item 4 — Remove Expand Xml / Collapse Xml CodeLens items

**Current state:** `provideCodeLenses()` in `commentCodeLensProvider.ts` emits two CodeLens per block:
1. "Expand Xml" / "Collapse Xml" toggle (lines 93–98)
2. Summary text preview

Auto-expand/collapse when cursor enters/leaves a comment already provides this functionality. The chevron in the gutter also serves. These explicit CodeLens items add noise.

**Change:** Remove CodeLens item #1 entirely. Keep all underlying fold state machinery (`foldState` map, `setFoldState`, `setAllFolded`, etc.) as it is used by the auto-expand/collapse decoration logic.

Note: `kat-comment-studio.toggleCommentFold` command registration in `extension.ts` and `package.json` can be removed **only if** no code paths outside of the deleted CodeLens call it. Verify before removing.

**Files:**
- `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts` — remove lines 93–98 (the Expand/Collapse CodeLens push)
- `package.json` — remove `toggleCommentFold` from `commands` array and any `menus` entries if it's no longer externally reachable
- `README.md` — remove documentation of Expand/Collapse CodeLens feature

---

### Item 5 — CodeLens summary: first `<para>` only

**Current state:** `getStrippedSummaryFromXml()` calls `extractPlainText(summaryNode)` which concatenates ALL `<para>` elements' text into the summary preview, producing an overly long codelens string.

**Change:** For the CodeLens summary preview only, truncate content at the first `<para>` boundary:
1. If there is text content **before** the first `<para>` child — use that text only.
2. Else if there is at least one `<para>` child — use the text content of the **first** `<para>` only.
3. Else — use all content as before (no `<para>` elements present).

**Implementation:** Add a new exported function `getFirstParagraphText(summaryElement: XmlElement): string` (or add an `options` parameter to `extractPlainText`) in `commentRenderer.ts`. Update `getStrippedSummaryFromXml()` to call it, or create a separate `getStrippedSummaryForCodeLens()` that `commentCodeLensProvider.ts` calls instead.

The hover popup (`renderToMarkdown`) is unaffected — it should still show the full summary with all `<para>` elements.

**Files:**
- `src/rendering/commentRenderer.ts` — add `extractFirstParagraphText()` helper, wire into `getStrippedSummaryFromXml()`
- `src/rendering/commentCodeLensProvider.ts` — no change needed if `getStrippedSummary()` is updated; update import if a new function is added
- `test/` — add tests for first-para truncation (text before first para, first-para-only, no-para cases)

---

## Implementation Order

1. Item 1 (trivial config change — do first)
2. Item 4 (remove codelens items — clean, no model changes)
3. Item 3 (rename + confirmation — isolated to one command file)
4. Item 5 (para extraction — isolated to renderer + codelens)
5. Item 2 (See Also table — largest change, touches types + renderer + rendering pipeline)

When all items are complete and `npm run compile` + `npm test` pass, update this file and all Phase Work headers to show **Complete** before committing.

---

## Notes

- **toggleCommentFold command**: Confirm during Item 4 whether `decorationManager` or any other code calls `kat-comment-studio.toggleCommentFold` programmatically before deciding to remove its `package.json` registration.
- **cref command URI**: `command:workbench.action.quickOpen?%5B%22%23{TypeName}%22%5D` requires `isTrusted: true` on the MarkdownString. Verify this is already set before implementation.
- **Safe CSS color**: `nameLink` in the SeeAlso table name cell must be validated — it originates from the XML doc comment so it's developer-controlled (not user input), but confirm no injection risk.
- All changed settings must be documented in README. Run `npm run compile` + `npm test` after each item.
